checklist.question: Did receivables grow materially faster than revenue across consecutive periods?

evidence.location: 10-K FY2014 comparative financial statements (Assets - AccountsReceivableNetCurrent; Revenues)

evidence.quote: AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=305151161.55 (2014-03-31) vs 234781472.05 (2013-03-31), +30.0% YoY; Revenues quarterly Jan-Mar 2014=563090858.88 vs Jan-Mar 2013=460726957.33, +22.2% YoY
evidence.location: 10-Q period 2014-03-31 balance sheet and income statement

checklist.question: Did non-current 'soft' assets (intangibles, other assets, capitalized items) grow materially faster than the business overall?

evidence.quote: IntangibleAssetsNetExcludingGoodwill=200144646.37 (2010-06-30) -> 658109789.61 (2014-06-30), a 3.29x increase, while Revenues grew from 899061400.81 (FY2010) to 2175520554.86 (FY2014), a 2.42x increase
evidence.location: 10-Q period 2014-06-30 balance sheet; annual Revenues series from FY2010 and FY2014 10-Ks

evidence.quote: Goodwill=520406991.87 (2010-06-30) -> 1145908384.00 (2014-06-30), a 2.20x increase, roughly in line with revenue growth of 2.42x

checklist.question: Is there a persistent divergence between net income and operating cash flow?

evidence.location: 10-K FY2014 cash flow statement and income statement, comparative years

checklist.question: Do inventory levels or gross margins show trends inconsistent with revenue?
Is the earnings trajectory unusually smooth relative to the volatility of revenue and cash flow?

evidence.quote: Quarterly NetIncomeLoss shows meaningful variability with recognizable seasonality: 16552701.40 (Q1 FY2013), 31943703.38 (Q2 FY2013), 41129210.15 (Q3 FY2013), then 27936345.49 (Q1 FY2014), 41650459.62 (Q2 FY2014), 35599520.93 (Q3 FY2014)

checklist.question: Did liability, allowance, or reserve balances decline (or fail to grow) despite growing business activity?

evidence.quote: AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent=2688071.43 (2012-06-30) / AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=168372672.47 (2012-06-30) = 1.60% coverage, declining to AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent=1602135.02 (2014-06-30) / AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=290844075.63 (2014-06-30) = 0.55% coverage
evidence.location: 10-Q period 2014-06-30 and 10-K FY2013 balance sheets (allowance and AR disclosures)

checklist.question: Does the filing chronology show irregularities (late filings/NT forms, amendments, unusual 8-K frequency)?

evidence.quote: NT 10-K filing_date=2007-08-29; NT 10-Q filing_date=2007-11-09; 10-K/A filings on 2003-10-03, 2005-11-02, 2008-03-26, 2008-10-28; recurring CORRESP/UPLOAD (SEC comment letter) correspondence in 2009, 2011-2012, and 2014

checklist.question: Is the provided data sufficient in coverage and length for the above assessments?

evidence.quote: Quarterly and annual XBRL data points span from FY2009 (2008-07-01 to 2009-06-30) through Q3 FY2015 (2015-01-01 to 2015-03-31) across Revenues, AccountsReceivableNetCurrent, InventoryNet, Goodwill, IntangibleAssetsNetExcludingGoodwill, NetIncomeLoss, and cash flow statement line items

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Under-provisioning of the allowance for doubtful accounts relative to a rapidly growing receivables base, which would understate bad debt expense and overstate net receivables and net income.

rationale_evidence.quote: AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent=2688071.43 (2012-06-30) vs AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=168372672.47 (2012-06-30) = 1.60% coverage; AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent=1602135.02 (2014-06-30) vs AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=290844075.63 (2014-06-30) = 0.55% coverage
rationale_evidence.location: 10-Q period 2014-06-30 and prior 10-K balance sheet allowance disclosures

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Receivables growing faster than revenue in certain periods (e.g., FY2013 annual and Q3 FY2014 YoY) could indicate aggressive revenue recognition timing, extended customer payment terms, or channel stuffing that inflates reported revenue/receivables ahead of cash collection.

rationale_evidence.location: 10-K FY2014 comparative balance sheet and income statement

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Rapid, acquisition-driven growth in intangible assets (outpacing revenue growth) raises risk that purchase price allocations or subsequent impairment testing understate impairment charges, keeping carrying values elevated relative to underlying business performance.

rationale_evidence.quote: IntangibleAssetsNetExcludingGoodwill=200144646.37 (2010-06-30) -> 658109789.61 (2014-06-30), a 3.29x increase, versus Revenues growth of 2.42x over the same period (899061400.81 FY2010 to 2175520554.86 FY2014)
rationale_evidence.location: 10-Q period 2014-06-30 balance sheet; annual Revenues series

top_signals: Declining allowance-for-doubtful-accounts coverage ratio (from ~1.6% of AR in FY2012 to ~0.55% in FY2014) despite AR nearly doubling
Receivables growth materially outpacing revenue growth in FY2013 (annual) and Q3 FY2014 (YoY)
Intangible assets ex-goodwill growing ~3.3x vs revenue growth of ~2.4x over FY2010-FY2014, reflecting aggressive M&A-driven balance sheet expansion

Historical pattern of late filings (NT 10-K/NT 10-Q in 2007) and multiple 10-K/A amendments plus recurring SEC comment-letter correspondence (2009, 2011-2012, 2014) indicating elevated regulatory scrutiny
Operating cash flow consistently exceeding net income (positive quality signal, partially offsetting other concerns)
